text.title: Ollama Python Client Patterns — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the ollama-python-client methodology. Sources: Ollama Python library (PyPI: ollama); Ollama HTTP API reference; OpenTelemetry Python SDK docs.

rule.statement: Wrap every call in retry-with-exponential-backoff (3 attempts, jitter); Ollama drops connections under load.
rule.rationale: Local Ollama under load returns 5xx from the proxy; without retries, agent steps fail randomly and traces look like model regressions.

rule.statement: Use streaming (`stream=True`) for any response > 500 tokens; bin-pack non-streaming calls only when the consumer cannot incrementally process.
rule.rationale: Streaming reduces p99 latency to first token by 5–10× and lets the agent abort early on detected garbage.

rule.statement: Parse JSON-mode responses through a Pydantic model; never trust raw dict access.
rule.rationale: Pydantic catches schema drift the moment Ollama returns a slightly different field name; raw dict access defers the failure to a NoneType.

rule.statement: Issue a warm-up request when the client process starts (single short prompt, single token); never let the first user request pay the load cost.
rule.rationale: Model load is 5–30 s; deferring it to the first user request makes the entire system look broken from outside.

rule.statement: Emit OpenTelemetry spans for every client call: model id, prompt id, latency, tokens-in, tokens-out, error class.
rule.rationale: Without traces, Ollama performance regressions are unreproducible; quantisation makes runs non-deterministic.
